tools.name: pr_view
tools.description: View pull request details including title, body, state, author, labels, reviewers, and file change stats. Use for understanding PR context, checking review decisions, or getting PR description content. Pass 'fields' for efficient JSON selection (e.g. 'title,body,state,reviews'). Omit 'number' to view the PR for the current branch.

number.description: Pull request number. Omit to use the PR associated with the current branch.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Defaults to the repo configured in .mcp-gh-tooling.json.

fields.description: Comma-separated JSON fields to return (e.g. 'title,body,state,reviews,comments,files'). Returns formatted text output when omitted.

comments.description: Include PR comments in output. Only applies when 'fields' is not set.

jq_filter.description: jq expression to filter/transform the output. Syntax is validated before execution. Most useful when 'fields' is also set for JSON output.

max_lines.description: Return only the first N lines of output (head -n N). Useful for long PR descriptions.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: pr_diff
tools.description: Get the unified diff (code changes) for a pull request. Use to inspect actual code modifications. Optionally filter to a single file path with 'file'. Use 'name_only' to list only changed file names without the diff content.

file.description: Limit diff to this specific file path (e.g. 'src/Core/Content/Product/ProductEntity.php').

name_only.description: List only changed file names, not the diff content.

max_lines.description: Return only the first N lines of output (head -n N). Useful for large diffs.

tail_lines.description: Return only the last N lines of output. Applied after max_lines if both are set.

grep_pattern.description: Filter output lines matching this extended regex (grep -E). Non-matching lines are omitted. Exit 1 (no matches) is treated as success with empty output.

grep_context_before.description: Lines of context to include before each grep match (-B).

grep_context_after.description: Lines of context to include after each grep match (-A).

grep_invert.description: Return lines that do NOT match grep_pattern (-v).

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: pr_list
tools.description: List pull requests with optional filters. Use to find PRs by author, state, search query, or branch. Supports JSON output for structured data.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

jq_filter.description: jq expression to filter/transform the output. Syntax is validated before execution. Most useful when 'fields' is also set for JSON output.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: pr_checks
tools.description: View the CI/CD status checks for a pull request. Shows check names, status (queued/in_progress/completed), and conclusions (success/failure/skipped). Use to quickly assess if a PR's CI is passing.

max_lines.description: Return only the first N lines of output (head -n N). Useful when there are many CI checks.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: pr_comments
tools.description: Get inline review comments (code-level comments on specific lines) for a pull request. These are reviewer annotations on the diff, not general conversation comments. Use to read detailed reviewer feedback on specific code lines. Returns paginated JSON by default.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Required if no default repo configured.

jq_filter.description: jq expression to filter/transform output (e.g. '.[] | {path, body, line, user: .user.login}'). Syntax is validated before execution.

max_lines.description: Return only the first N lines of output (head -n N). Useful when there are many comment threads.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: pr_reviews
tools.description: Get the reviews for a pull request. Returns reviewer decisions (APPROVED, CHANGES_REQUESTED, COMMENTED, DISMISSED) and review body text. Use to understand the review history and current approval status.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Required if no default repo configured.

jq_filter.description: jq expression to filter/transform output (e.g. '.[] | select(.state == "CHANGES_REQUESTED") | {user: .user.login, body}'). Syntax is validated before execution.

max_lines.description: Return only the first N lines of output (head -n N). Useful when there are many reviews.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: pr_files
tools.description: Get the list of files changed in a pull request with patch content. Default output shows filename, status, additions, and deletions. Use 'jq_filter' to extract patch content for specific files (e.g. filter by filename to get the actual code diff).

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Required if no default repo configured.

jq_filter.description: jq expression to filter files. Default: '.[] | {filename, status, additions, deletions}'. Example: '.[] | select(.filename | contains("Migration")) | {filename, patch}'. Syntax is validated before execution.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: pr_commits
tools.description: Get the commit history for a pull request. Returns commit SHAs and first-line commit messages by default. Use to understand what changes were made across commits in a PR.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Required if no default repo configured.

jq_filter.description: jq expression to transform output. Default: '.[] | {sha: .sha[0:10], message: (.commit.message | split("\n")[0])}'. Syntax is validated before execution.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: issue_view
tools.description: View a GitHub issue including title, body, state, labels, assignees, and milestone. Use to understand bug reports, feature requests, or task descriptions. Pass 'with_comments' to include discussion thread.

with_comments.description: Include issue comments in text output. Only applies when 'fields' is not set.

jq_filter.description: jq expression to filter/transform the output. Syntax is validated before execution. Most useful when 'fields' is also set for JSON output.

max_lines.description: Return only the first N lines of output (head -n N). Useful for long issue bodies.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: issue_list
tools.description: List GitHub issues with optional filters. Use to find issues by search query, state, or label.

jq_filter.description: jq expression to filter/transform the output. Syntax is validated before execution. Most useful when 'fields' is also set for JSON output.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: run_view
tools.description: View the status and summary of a GitHub Actions workflow run. Returns run state (queued/in_progress/completed), conclusion (success/failure/cancelled), and job list. Use to check if CI passed and identify which jobs failed.

run_id.description: GitHub Actions workflow run ID (numeric, e.g. 21534190745).

jq_filter.description: jq expression to filter/transform the output. Syntax is validated before execution. Most useful when 'fields' is also set for JSON output.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: run_list
tools.description: List recent GitHub Actions workflow runs. Use to find runs for a specific branch or to see recent CI history.

jq_filter.description: jq expression to filter/transform the output. Syntax is validated before execution. Most useful when 'fields' is also set for JSON output.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: run_logs
tools.description: Get logs for a GitHub Actions workflow run. Returns failed step logs by default (recommended for CI debugging). Set 'failed_only' to false to get all step logs. Use 'max_lines'/'tail_lines' to limit output, or 'grep_pattern' to filter for specific error messages.

failed_only.description: Return only failed step logs (recommended). Set to false to get all step logs.

max_lines.description: Return only the first N lines of output (head -n N). Applied before tail_lines if both are set.

tail_lines.description: Return only the last N lines of output. Applied after max_lines if both are set.

grep_pattern.description: Filter output lines matching this extended regex (grep -E). Non-matching lines are omitted. Exit 1 (no matches) is treated as success with empty output.

grep_context_before.description: Lines of context to include before each grep match (-B).

grep_context_after.description: Lines of context to include after each grep match (-A).

grep_invert.description: Return lines that do NOT match grep_pattern (-v).

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: job_view
tools.description: Get details for a specific GitHub Actions job by job ID. Returns job status, conclusion, runner info, and individual step statuses with timing. Use after run_view to inspect a specific failing job.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Required if no default repo configured.

jq_filter.description: jq expression to filter output (e.g. '.steps[] | select(.conclusion == "failure") | {name, conclusion, number}'). Syntax is validated before execution.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: job_logs
tools.description: Get the raw log output for a specific GitHub Actions job by job ID. More targeted than run_logs - use when you know the specific job that failed. Use 'max_lines'/'tail_lines' to limit output, or 'grep_pattern' to filter for specific error messages.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Required if no default repo configured.

max_lines.description: Return only the first N lines of output (head -n N). Applied before tail_lines if both are set.

tail_lines.description: Return only the last N lines of output. Applied after max_lines if both are set.

grep_pattern.description: Filter output lines matching this extended regex (grep -E). Non-matching lines are omitted. Exit 1 (no matches) is treated as success with empty output.

grep_context_before.description: Lines of context to include before each grep match (-B).

grep_context_after.description: Lines of context to include after each grep match (-A).

grep_invert.description: Return lines that do NOT match grep_pattern (-v).

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: job_annotations
tools.description: Get check annotations (inline error/warning messages pinned to code lines) for a CI check run. Useful for getting PHPStan errors, ESLint violations, or test failure details directly from GitHub's annotation system.

check_run_id.description: GitHub check run ID (same as job ID for Actions jobs, e.g. 62056364818).

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Required if no default repo configured.

jq_filter.description: jq expression to filter annotations (e.g. '.[] | {path, start_line, message, annotation_level}'). Syntax is validated before execution.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: commit_info
tools.description: Get details for a specific git commit: changed files list and/or commit message. Optionally also fetch associated pull requests for the commit. Use to understand what a specific SHA changed.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Required if no default repo configured.

fields.description: What information to return. 'files': list of changed filenames. 'message': commit message. 'files_and_message': both (default).

jq_filter.description: Custom jq expression overriding 'fields' (e.g. '.files | [.[] | .filename]'). Syntax is validated before execution.

include_pulls.description: Also fetch pull requests associated with this commit.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search for GitHub issues or pull requests using a text query. Use to find related work, historical context, or PRs that fixed similar problems. Set 'type' to 'issues' for issues or 'prs' for pull requests.

query.description: Search query text (e.g. 'NEXT-3412', 'attribute entity', 'custom field translated').

type.description: Search for issues or pull requests.

repo.description: Limit search to this repository in 'owner/repo' format.

state.description: Filter by open or closed state.

jq_filter.description: jq expression to filter/transform the output. Syntax is validated before execution. Most useful when 'fields' is also set for JSON output.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.

tools.name: api
tools.description: Execute a raw GitHub REST API call using gh api. Use as an escape hatch when specific tools don't cover your case. Common endpoints: 'repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{n}/files', 'repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{n}/timeline', 'repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/comments/{id}', 'search/issues'. Automatically handles authentication.

endpoint.description: API endpoint path relative to https://api.github.com/ (e.g. 'repos/shopware/shopware/pulls/14642/comments').

jq_filter.description: jq expression to filter/transform the JSON response. Syntax is validated before execution.

paginate.description: Fetch all pages of paginated results.

fields.description: Comma-separated fields for --jq '{field1, field2}' selection. Use 'jq_filter' for complex expressions.

max_lines.description: Return only the first N lines of output (head -n N). Useful for large API responses.

tail_lines.description: Return only the last N lines of output. Applied after max_lines if both are set.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced; the tool returns empty output instead of an error message. Useful when the resource may not exist.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit). When provided, the tool always succeeds and the fallback text is returned instead of the error.
